commit.message:
feat(api): backfill paddle_customer_id across all webhook processors

Add shared backfillPaddleCustomerId() helper in utils.ts that checks
if paddle_customer_id is null on user profile and fills it from the
Paddle event payload. Called from all 9 subscription and transaction
webhook processors as a safety net — ensures the customer portal
button works even if the original subscription.created webhook missed.

Processors updated:
- subscription-updated, subscription-canceled, subscription-paused
- subscription-resumed, subscription-activated, subscription-past-due
- transaction-completed, transaction-payment-failed, transaction-refunded

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: 08_implementations — colocate concept-specific impl notes with their layers

- Rename 08_reference_implementations → 08_implementations
- Move 10 concept-specific model impl notes to home layers:
  - linear_models, tree_ensembles, kernel_methods → 03_modeling/01_supervised_learning/
  - neural_network → 03_modeling/04_deep_learning/01_mlp_and_representation_learning/
  - graphical_model → 03_modeling/03_probabilistic_models/01_graphical_models/
  - probabilistic_models → 03_modeling/03_probabilistic_models/03_bayesian_modeling/
  - unsupervised_learning → 03_modeling/02_unsupervised_learning/01_clustering/
  - time_series → 03_modeling/05_time_series/01_classical_forecasting/
  - interpretability → 03_modeling/07_evaluation_and_model_selection/
  - transformer_finetuning → 06_ai_engineering/05_finetuning/

- Rename 02_system_patterns → 01_system_patterns
- Rename 03_end_to_end_examples → 02_end_to_end_examples
- Repair all ~84 affected wikilinks and frontmatter

- Update README: layer description for 08_implementations

commit.message:
docs: update README to reflect Phase 1 completion (CRITICAL audit finding)

Update README.md to accurately document implemented functionality:
- Status: Changed from 'Phase 0' to 'Phase 1 (85% Complete)'
- Current Functionality: Comprehensive list of implemented features:
  * Protocol Layer: Wayland client (6 packages), X11 client (4 packages)
  * Rendering Layer: Software 2D rasterizer (5 packages)
  * UI Framework: Widget layer with layout system (3 packages)
- Architecture: Detailed 5-layer architecture documentation
- Known Limitations: Document integration gaps and missing optimizations
- Contributing: Updated to reflect Phase 1 status with priority tasks

This addresses the CRITICAL finding from AUDIT-2026-03-07.md where
README claimed only Phase 0 implementation while codebase contains
97.7% of Phase 1 features (3,250 LOC protocol, 1,550 LOC rasterizer,
700 LOC UI framework).

Fixes: AUDIT-2026-03-07.md - CRITICAL item #1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add open source GitHub Actions repo patterns and conventions

Analyzed 6 repos (contributors, evergreen, issue-metrics, stale-repos,
cleanowners, measure-innersource) and documented shared architecture:
- Flat Python module layout with common module split
- 5-tool linting chain (flake8/isort/pylint/mypy/black)
- pytest with 80% coverage minimum
- Docker patterns (python:3.14-slim, pinned SHAs)
- CI/CD workflow standards
- github3-py + dual auth + env.py conventions
- Conventional commits, fail-fast validation, dual output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Updated README Mermaid diagram and pin table for 3-pin buzzer

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add #jump(n, relative: bool) as unified animation control; redefine #pause/#meanwhile as sugar

* Initial plan

* Fix #pause combined with #uncover/#only/#alternatives behaving unexpectedly

- Fix A: touying-fn-wrapper items always escape the pause zone (always go
  to result instead of hidden-parts), allowing uncover/only/alternatives
  to handle their own subslide visibility regardless of surrounding pauses.

- Fix B: Two-pass inner parse for all container types (sequences, styled
  elements, list items, table/grid/stack, box/block/etc., terms items,
  columns, place, rotate). When a container would be hidden by a pause
  zone but contains fn-wrappers (next-last-subslide > 0), re-run the
  inner parse with the outer need-cover so non-fn-wrapper content is
  properly covered by the inner mechanism while fn-wrappers escape.

- Add test cases in tests/features/animation/test.typ:
  == Pause + Uncover/Only in List (reproduces the exact issue report)
  == Pause + Uncover/Only Inline
  == Pause + Alternatives Inline

- Add 10 new reference images (pages 22-31) for the new test slides.

Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update tests

* Add step(n) and goto(n) as generalized animation controls

- `#step(n)` advances the subslide counter by n relative to current position;
  `#pause` is now defined as `step(1)` (syntactic sugar)
- `#goto(n)` jumps to absolute subslide n;
  `#meanwhile` is now defined as `goto(1)` (syntactic sugar)
- Both functions validate that n is a positive integer
- Update all parsers (main content loop, table/grid cell wrapper, CeTZ/Fletcher
  reducer) to handle `touying-step` and `touying-goto` metadata kinds
- Export `step` and `goto` from src/exports.typ
- Add tests: step(1) == pause, step(2) skips subslide, goto(1) == meanwhile,
  goto(3) jumps to subslide 3, step/goto in CeTZ canvas
- Add 13 new reference images (pages 32-44)

Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace step(n)/goto(n) with unified jump(n, relative:) function

- Replace `step(n)` and `goto(n)` with a single `jump(n, relative: false)`
- `relative: true` (relative mode): advances counter by n (n != 0, can be negative)
- `relative: false` (absolute mode, default): flush hidden content, set counter to n (n >= 1)
- `#pause` = `jump(1, relative: true)`, `#meanwhile` = `jump(1)` (sugar)
- Update all 3 parsers to handle `touying-jump` kind with relative field
- Update exports: remove step/goto, add jump
- Update tests: section renamed "Jump", use jump() syntax
- Update reference images for pages 32-44

Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix negative relative jump not flushing hidden-parts at correct position

When jump(-1, relative: true) takes the subslide counter from a hidden
zone back into a visible zone, flush accumulated hidden-parts immediately
at that point (not deferred to the end) to preserve correct content order.

Fixes:
- _parse-touying-reducer: flush on re-entry to visible zone
- inner children loop in _parse-content-into-results-and-repetitions: same

Add test case for jump(-1, relative: true) in CeTZ canvas.

Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix max-repetitions not updated on positive relative jump before negative jump

When jump(+1, relative: true) raised repetitions to 2 and jump(-1, relative:
true) brought it back to 1, max-repetitions was never captured at the peak.
The final calc.max(1, 1)=1 caused only 1 subslide to be generated.

Fix: update max-repetitions immediately after every relative jump in both
_parse-touying-reducer and the inner children loop.

The "negative relative in CeTZ" test now correctly produces 2 subslides
(46 total pages, was 45 before this fix).

Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update tests

* fmt

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <318483724@qq.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve cycle-2 review issues (injection hardening across 30+ callsites)

- Fix JS injection in wait_for_event: use json_escape() for selector and event
- Fix systemic backslash bypass: add .replace('\\', "\\\\\\\\" ) before single-quote
  escaping at 69 callsites across 22 files
- Fix JS injection in block_requests: add backslash escaping for regex patterns,
  use json_escape() for resource_types entries
- Fix shell command injection in normal.rs: replace sh -c with pure Rust
  Command::new("ps") + String::contains() — no shell interpretation needed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Cycle 20: E2E browser test infra — fix Kestrel startup, login flow, BFF checkout gaps, and SignalR antiforgery (#177)

* Initial plan

* Fix E2E test infrastructure, BFF gaps, checkout bugs, and order confirmation bug

- Add BFF proxy endpoints to Storefront.Api for checkout steps:
  ProvideCheckoutShippingAddress, SelectCheckoutShippingMethod, ProvideCheckoutPaymentMethod
- Add GetOrderView endpoint to Storefront.Api (fixes OrderConfirmation showing 'not found')
- Add GetOrderAsync to IOrdersClient interface and all stub implementations
- Fix OrdersClient.CompleteCheckoutAsync to parse orderId from response (not return checkoutId)
- Fix Checkout.razor: route all checkout steps through StorefrontApi BFF (remove Orders.Api bypass)
- Fix Checkout.razor: add MudStepper auto-advance with try/catch exception handling
- Fix Checkout.razor: add always-visible order summary sidebar with data-testid attributes
- Add full address fields to AddressSummary for shipping address resolution
- Update E2ETestFixture to use built-in UseKestrel() support (ASP.NET Core 10+)
- Add StubCustomerIdentityApiHandler for login stub in StorefrontWebKestrelFactory
- Rewrite StubOrdersClient (E2E) to reject tok_invalid payment token

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add diagnostic logging to Checkout.razor OnInitializedAsync; fix Login.razor ValidateAsync; fix LoginPage timing

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix MudSelect address option selection in CheckoutPage - use has-text locator after popup opens

The CI failure (7/8 tests timing out at 30s) was caused by
GetByRole(AriaRole.Option, { Name = "Home" }) not matching the
rendered MudSelectItem text "Home - 123 Main St, Seattle, WA 98101".

Fix: wait for the MudBlazor popup to open ([role='option'] visible),
then use [role='option']:has-text('Home') for partial text matching.
Also wait for AddressSelect to be visible before clicking to open it.

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: disable antiforgery on SignalR hub to fix E2E test failures

ASP.NET Core 10 enables antiforgery token validation on SignalR hubs
by default. WebSocket connections have built-in CORS protection that
already prevents CSRF attacks, making HTML form antiforgery redundant
for SignalR.

The E2E tests were seeing repeated 400 errors:
  'A valid antiforgery token was not provided with the request'

This caused SignalR negotiation to fail, breaking all real-time
order status update scenarios in the checkout flow E2E suite.

Fixes: Storefront.E2ETests SignalR connection establishment step

* docs: Add E2E Playwright testing skill document

Adds docs/skills/e2e-playwright-testing.md covering all patterns from
the Cycle 20 Storefront.E2ETests implementation:

- Real Kestrel servers via WebApplicationFactory (UseKestrel(0) ordering rule)
- Two-server architecture: Storefront.Web → Storefront.Api → stubs + TestContainers
- Page Object Model with data-testid selectors (MudBlazor-safe)
- MudSelect animated popover interaction pattern
- Login flow timing with Blazor forceLoad navigation race
- Stub coordination for deterministic IDs across cart→checkout boundary
- localStorage injection for pre-seeded browser state
- Wolverine InvokeMessageAndWaitAsync for SignalR E2E scenarios
- SignalR antiforgery fix for ASP.NET Core 10+
- Full Reqnroll hook lifecycle (BeforeTestRun / BeforeScenario / AfterScenario)
- Playwright tracing on failure
- WellKnownTestData and ScenarioContextKeys patterns
- Common pitfalls with concrete fixes
- Checklist for new E2E scenarios

Also updates docs/skills/README.md and CLAUDE.md to reference the new doc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SignalR antiforgery + add E2E Playwright skills doc; address code review feedback

- DisableAntiforgery() on SignalR hub (ASP.NET Core 10+ default breaks WS negotiation)
- Add explanatory comment for why antiforgery is disabled (code review feedback)
- Add docs/skills/e2e-playwright-testing.md (902 lines) covering Kestrel factory,
  POM/data-testid patterns, MudSelect popup, login timing, stub coordination,
  localStorage injection, Wolverine message injection, SignalR antiforgery,
  Playwright tracing, and WellKnownTestData patterns
- Update docs/skills/README.md and CLAUDE.md to reference new doc
- Add CI browser caching guidance and SlowMo documentation to new skill doc

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(keymaps): guard nil changes in find_hunk_at_cursor

find_hunk_at_cursor() crashed with 'attempt to get length of field
changes (a nil value)' when stored_diff_result existed but its
.changes field was nil (e.g. after single-pane views or during
async transitions).

Add a nil check before accessing #diff_result.changes.

fix: remove 3 non-deterministic HMP commands; add system_reset op; disk guard

- Replace 'info registers', 'info cpu', 'info irq' in HMP_COMMANDS with
  deterministic alternatives ('info lapic', 'info kvm', 'info version').
  These three commands contained CPU pc= values and IRQ timer counts that
  varied with concurrent SeaBIOS execution, causing inflated corpus add rate.
- Add op 16 (system_reset): exercises QEMU machine reset code paths, a known
  source of use-after-free and re-initialization bugs. Live hotplug resources
  survive reset and continue to be exercised afterwards.
- Update ChunkOpMutator to use below(17) to cover all 17 ops uniformly.
- Add 2 new seeds: seed-system-reset.bin and seed-reset-with-device.bin
  (device_add → system_reset → device_del stress sequence).
- Add disk guard to run_fuzzer.sh: wipes queue if sync/ exceeds 10 GB.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial plan (#173)

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 20 core system design concept write-ups

New concepts/ directory with 7,252 lines across 20 standalone
concept pages + README index. Each includes Mermaid diagrams,
comparison tables, and cross-references to full design docs.

Fundamentals (5):
- Load Balancing (318 lines) — L4/L7, 8 algorithms, HA diagrams
- Caching (342 lines) — 4 patterns, eviction policies, stampede
- Database Sharding (308 lines) — 5 strategies, resharding
- Replication (365 lines) — single/multi/leaderless, failover
- CAP Theorem (383 lines) — triangle, PACELC, tunable consistency

Communication (5):
- Consistent Hashing (235 lines) — ring, virtual nodes, math
- Message Queues (291 lines) — P2P/pub-sub, delivery guarantees
- WebSockets (304 lines) — vs polling/SSE, scaling, heartbeat
- API Gateway (311 lines) — BFF, middleware chain, data/control plane
- Service Discovery (394 lines) — client/server-side, Consul/K8s

Data & Storage (3):
- Database Indexing (365 lines) — B-tree, LSM, composite, covering
- Data Partitioning (406 lines) — hash/range, rebalancing
- Eventual Consistency (450 lines) — CRDTs, vector clocks, quorum

Architecture (2):
- Microservices (490 lines) — saga pattern, bounded contexts, DDD
- Rate Limiting (594 lines) — 5 algorithms, Redis Lua, hybrid

Reliability (5):
- Scalability (310 lines) — vertical/horizontal, Amdahl's Law
- Fault Tolerance (338 lines) — circuit breaker, chaos engineering
- Monitoring (365 lines) — metrics/logs/traces, RED/USE/Golden
- Auth (393 lines) — OAuth2, JWT, RBAC/ABAC, mTLS
- CDN (257 lines) — pull/push, cache hierarchy, multi-CDN

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
clear_print_error: add uiop dialog-close signal + update behavioral rules

BambuStudio sends two commands when dismissing a print error dialog:
1. clean_print_error - clears the print_error value
2. uiop (system/dialog-close) - signals UI acknowledgment to the printer

Without the uiop signal the printer stays in UI-acknowledgment pending
state, causing any open BambuStudio session to re-raise print_error on
every push_status, overriding the clear.

Changes:
- clear_print_error() now calls printer.clean_print_error_uiop() after
  printer.clean_print_error(), using the new BPM method
- Dropped inline send_anything() + import json (replaced by BPM method)
- Updated docstring to document the two-command protocol
- behavioral_rules: added two-command clear protocol rule and
  gcode_state FAILED semantics after cancellation

Companion BPM change: adds CLEAN_PRINT_ERROR_UIOP_TEMPLATE, the
clean_print_error_uiop() method, and restores toJson() as a proper
standalone method (fixing a pre-existing regression from the
clean_print_error commit).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: On-device conversational search (RAG) — unified natural language & keyword search (#244)

* Initial plan

* chore: start RAG assistant implementation

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add on-device RAG conversational reference assistant

- Add ragSearch utility with TF-IDF scoring over all reference entries
- Add RagAssistantScreen with chat-style UI, result cards, and jump-to-section navigation
- Register RagAssistant route in AppNavigator
- Add chat-bubble icon button to SectionHeader for assistant access
- Add 29 unit tests covering tokenize, scoreEntry, createExcerpt, ragSearch

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: unify Search + RAG into single conversational screen; fix footer overflow

- Rewrite SearchScreen as a unified chat-style screen: tapping the section
  header opens one screen for both keyword queries and natural language questions
- On send, runs RAG (reference content) + keyword search (user data) in parallel
  and renders combined results as conversation messages with "Jump to section" cards
- Fix footer overflow: move paddingBottom: FOOTER_HEIGHT to the outer container,
  outside KeyboardAvoidingView, matching the codebase layout convention
- Revert SectionHeader to single-button (header tap → Search); remove separate
  RAG chat-bubble icon button
- Remove now-unused RagAssistant route and RagAssistantScreen import from AppNavigator

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: move search input to top of screen with section-header styling

The input bar now sits at the top of SearchScreen, in the same position
as the SectionHeader — centered, 80% wide, borderWidth 2, borderRadius 8,
fontSize 20 / fontWeight 800, same SECONDARY_ACCENT + TOAST_BROWN colors.
A HorizontalRule follows the input, matching SectionHeader layout exactly.
The send button appears inline inside the input box only when text has been
entered. Chat message history scrolls below the rule, leaving the input
always visible at the top.

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: scroll to user message; expand RAG index to all hardcoded content

Scroll behavior:
- onLayout tracks each message's Y offset in a Map<id, y> ref
- After results arrive, scrollTo(userMsgY - 8) so the question is near
  the top and the user can scroll down through results at their own pace
- Falls back to scrollToEnd if layout isn't recorded yet

RAG index expansion (Morse and all hardcoded modules):
- Import scenarioCards.json (24 entries) via scenarioCardToEntry adapter
  (first_5_minutes/first_hour/first_day → steps; immediate_risks → do_not)
- 5 synthetic ReferenceEntryType entries: Morse Code, NATO Phonetic,
  Radio Frequency References, Ground-to-Air Signals, Digital Whistle
- Each synthetic entry has rich tags/steps for natural language matching
  and a related_screen pointing to its app screen

Smart navigation:
- handleJumpToEntry uses entry.related_screen when present
- Button label uses related_screen_label (e.g. "Open Morse Code")
- SearchScreenNavigationProp extended with all synthetic screen names

Tests: 6 new tests for Morse, Radio Frequencies, Ground-to-Air, scenario
cards, and related_screen correctness (594 total, all passing)

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reorder imports in ragSearch test

Move ReferenceEntryType import before the utility imports in __tests__/ragSearch.test.ts to improve import ordering and consistency (addresses lint/import-order preferences).

* feat: expand RAG index to cover every tool in every module

Every tool screen that didn't previously have a synthetic index entry now
does, so natural language queries like "unit conversion" or "lunar cycles"
return the right result.

New synthetic entries:
- Core: Unit Conversion, Lunar Cycles, Barometric Pressure, Sun Times,
  Device Status, Flashlight, Voice Log
- Navigation: Map, Star Map & Celestial Navigation
- Prepper: Emergency Planner

All entries have rich tags, descriptive steps, and a related_screen so
the "Open …" button navigates directly to the correct tool screen.

SearchScreen navigation type updated with all new screen names.
8 new tests added (602 total, all passing).

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reformat ragSearch tests to wrap long lines

Reformat __tests__/ragSearch.test.ts to wrap long string arguments and expect expressions across multiple lines for improved readability (line-length / prettier style). No functional changes to test behavior.

* refactor: remove unused RagAssistantScreen

The screen was an orphan — not imported or registered in AppNavigator.
The unified SearchScreen covers all search + RAG functionality.

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: cancel pending setTimeout timers when SearchScreen unmounts

Store both setTimeout IDs in a ref and clear them in a useEffect cleanup
to prevent "setState on unmounted component" warnings when the user
navigates away while a search is in progress.

Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jason-shprintz <119976085+jason-shprintz@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: cursor visibility indicator on video snippet list

- Shows MousePointer2Off icon when cursor is hidden (default)
- Shows MousePointer icon when cursor is visible
- Tooltip explains the state on hover

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Use centralized notify-discord composite action

Replace inline Discord notification step with reusable composite action
from magemonkeystudio/.github. Supports multiple webhook endpoints via
the org-level WEBHOOK_URLS secret (newline-separated).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add `install` command and workflow dispatch actions (#133)

* Initial plan

* Add install command, workflow templates, and plan workflow

- New `wreck-it install` CLI command with --work-dir option
- Creates .wreck-it/config.toml with engineering-team template
- Creates .github/workflows/ralph.yml and plan.yml
- New .github/workflows/install.yml dispatch action (owner/repo inputs)
- New .github/workflows/plan.yml dispatch action for this repo
- 7 unit tests for install module

Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: fix eval injection, use proper error handling

- Replace eval-based shell commands with positional args (set --)
  in plan.yml templates to prevent command injection
- Replace expect() with ok_or_else() for proper error propagation

Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>
